analysis.summary: Grafana is an open-source monitoring and observability platform that provides data visualization, dashboards, alerting, and multi-datasource query capabilities. The system implements a microservice-oriented monorepo architecture with a Go backend providing REST APIs and a TypeScript/React frontend for visualization rendering. It features a comprehensive plugin system for datasources and panels, real-time alerting with notification routing, and supports querying across heterogeneous data sources including Prometheus, InfluxDB, Elasticsearch, and SQL databases.
capabilities: Renders interactive dashboards with customizable panels and visualizations (public/app/features/dashboard/)
Provides REST API endpoints for dashboard management, data source configuration, and user authentication (pkg/api/)
Implements pluggable data source architecture supporting Prometheus, InfluxDB, CloudWatch, and 150+ integrations (pkg/plugins/)
Executes real-time alerting rules with configurable notification channels and routing trees (pkg/services/alerting/)
Processes time-series queries with template variable substitution and query transformations (pkg/tsdb/)
Manages multi-tenant organizations with role-based access control and team permissions (pkg/services/user/, pkg/services/org/)
Stores dashboard definitions, user preferences, and alert configurations in SQL databases (pkg/services/sqlstore/)
Handles OAuth2, LDAP, SAML, and API key authentication with configurable providers (pkg/services/auth/)
Provides live data streaming and real-time dashboard updates via WebSocket connections (pkg/services/live/)
Implements annotation system for marking events and incidents on time-series graphs (pkg/services/annotations/)
Supports dashboard templating with dynamic variable resolution and cascading filters (pkg/services/dashboards/)
Executes background jobs for alert evaluation, cleanup tasks, and data source health checks (pkg/services/scheduler/)
Provides plugin development SDK with TypeScript APIs for custom visualizations and data sources (packages/grafana-data/)
Implements search functionality across dashboards, data sources, and alerts with full-text indexing (pkg/services/search/)
Manages dashboard versioning with diff comparison and rollback capabilities (pkg/services/dashboards/database/)
Provides unified alerting system with rule evaluation, notification policies, and silence management (pkg/services/ngalert/)
Implements data source proxy for secure credential management and CORS handling (pkg/api/datasource_proxy.go)
Supports dashboard provisioning via configuration files and GitOps workflows (pkg/services/provisioning/)
Provides metrics collection and performance monitoring with Prometheus integration (pkg/infra/metrics/)
Implements plugin signature verification and security validation for third-party extensions (pkg/plugins/plugincontext/)
Manages library panels for reusable visualization components across dashboards (pkg/services/librarypanels/)
Provides correlation analysis between different data sources and metric relationships (apps/correlations/)
Implements folder-based dashboard organization with hierarchical permissions (pkg/services/folder/)
Supports dashboard sharing with public URLs and embed capabilities (pkg/api/short_url.go)
Provides query history and favorites for improved user experience (pkg/services/query/)
use_cases: DevOps teams use it to monitor application performance, infrastructure health, and set up alerting for system anomalies
Site reliability engineers use it to create operational dashboards for incident response and capacity planning
Data engineers use it to visualize data pipeline metrics and monitor ETL job performance across multiple data sources
Business analysts use it to create executive dashboards combining business metrics from databases, APIs, and cloud services
Security teams use it to monitor security events, analyze logs, and track compliance metrics in centralized dashboards
analysis.target_audience: DevOps engineers, Site reliability engineers, System administrators, Data engineers, Security analysts
technical_stack: Redis for caching and session storage (referenced in configurations)
CUE for schema validation and configuration (cue.mod/)
Jest for unit testing (package.json test scripts)
ESLint and Prettier for code formatting (eslint.config.js)
sdk_languages: Go (plugin development SDK and backend data sources)
auth_methods: OAuth2 with configurable providers including Google, GitHub, GitLab (pkg/services/auth/)
API key authentication for service accounts (pkg/services/apikey/)
Role-based access control with organization and team permissions (pkg/services/accesscontrol/)
integrations: Prometheus for metrics collection via prometheus client libraries (devenv/docker/blocks/prometheus_*/go.mod)
PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite databases via GORM ORM (pkg/services/sqlstore/)
Redis for caching and session management (configuration references)
LDAP directories for user authentication (pkg/services/ldap/)
SMTP servers for email notifications (pkg/services/notifications/)
Elasticsearch for log data and search (data source plugins)
InfluxDB for time-series data storage (data source plugins)
AWS CloudWatch, Azure Monitor, Google Cloud Monitoring (cloud integrations)
Slack, PagerDuty, Webhook notifications for alerting (pkg/services/alerting/)
Kubernetes API for container monitoring (data source plugins)

architecture.pattern: monolith with microservice apps - single deployable unit with modular internal services
components: Backend API server (pkg/api/) - REST endpoints and business logic
Frontend React application (public/app/) - dashboard rendering and user interface
Plugin system (pkg/plugins/) - extensible data sources and visualizations
Alerting engine (pkg/services/alerting/) - rule evaluation and notification routing
Data source proxy (pkg/api/datasource_proxy.go) - secure credential management
Live streaming service (pkg/services/live/) - real-time WebSocket updates
Search indexing service (pkg/services/search/) - full-text search capabilities
architecture.data_flow: HTTP requests hit the Go backend API, which authenticates users, validates permissions, and routes requests to appropriate services. Dashboard queries are proxied to configured data sources with credential injection. Results are transformed and cached before being sent to the React frontend for visualization rendering. Real-time updates flow through WebSocket connections managed by the live service.
architecture.persistence: Primary data stored in SQL databases (PostgreSQL/MySQL/SQLite) via SQLAlchemy-like ORM, with Redis used for session management and caching. Plugin configurations and dashboard definitions persisted as JSON in database tables.
architecture.concurrency_model: Go goroutines for concurrent request handling with context-based cancellation. Background services use worker pools for alert evaluation and scheduled tasks. Frontend uses React concurrent features for UI updates.
architecture.communication_patterns: HTTP REST APIs between frontend and backend, WebSocket for live updates, HTTP proxy for data source queries, internal Go interfaces for service communication
architecture.plugin_extension_model: Plugin architecture supports data source and panel plugins via TypeScript/Go SDKs. Plugins loaded dynamically with signature verification and sandboxed execution environment.

primary_entities: Dashboard - JSON document with panels, queries, and layout configuration
DataSource - Connection configuration for external data systems
User - Authentication and authorization entity with org/team membership
Alert Rule - Query-based conditions with notification routing
Panel - Individual visualization component within dashboards
Annotation - Time-based markers and events on dashboards
Folder - Hierarchical organization for dashboards with permissions
storage_backends: SQL databases (PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite) for primary data storage
Redis for session storage and caching
File system for plugin assets and static files
data_model.schema_patterns: Relational database with JSON columns for flexible configuration storage. Dashboard definitions stored as JSON documents with relational metadata for search and permissions.
data_model.migration_strategy: SQL migrations managed via custom migration system in pkg/services/sqlstore/migrations/

algorithms.problem_type: Real-time data visualization and monitoring with multi-source query federation and alert evaluation
core_patterns: Plugin architecture for extensible data sources and visualizations
Proxy pattern for secure data source access with credential injection
Observer pattern for real-time dashboard updates via WebSocket
Strategy pattern for multiple authentication providers
Factory pattern for plugin instantiation and management
Repository pattern for data access abstraction
algorithms.complexity_indicators: Large monorepo with 20,000+ files, complex plugin system, multi-tenant architecture, real-time streaming capabilities
key_algorithms: Query result caching and invalidation strategies (pkg/services/query/)
Alert rule evaluation with configurable intervals (pkg/services/ngalert/)
Template variable resolution and query transformation (pkg/services/dashboards/)
Plugin signature verification and security validation (pkg/plugins/)
Time-series data aggregation and downsampling (frontend query processing)
algorithms.evidence: pkg/services/alerting/ contains rule evaluation logic, pkg/plugins/ implements plugin loading and validation, pkg/services/live/ manages WebSocket connections for real-time updates

security_model.authentication: Multi-provider authentication supporting OAuth2, LDAP, SAML, and API keys with configurable session management
security_model.authorization: Role-based access control with organization-level isolation, team-based permissions, and resource-specific access controls
security_model.data_protection: API key storage with hashing, credential proxy for secure data source access, configurable TLS for transport security
security_model.security_headers: Configurable CORS policies, CSP headers, and rate limiting for API endpoints
security_model.vulnerability_handling: Plugin signature verification, dependency scanning in CI/CD, security policy documentation (SECURITY.md)

infrastructure: Redis for caching and session storage

deployment.container_orchestration: Docker Compose for development (devenv/docker/), Kubernetes production deployment support
deployment.ci_cd: GitHub Actions for CI/CD pipeline with automated testing and releases (.github/workflows/)
deployment.environment_config: Environment variables, configuration files (conf/defaults.ini), and feature flags for runtime configuration
deployment.scaling_model: Horizontal scaling supported through stateless design with shared database and Redis backend

code_quality_signals.test_framework: Jest for unit tests, Playwright for E2E tests, Go testing framework
code_quality_signals.test_count_estimate: 3133 test files found across the repository
code_quality_signals.documentation_quality: well-documented with extensive README, contributing guidelines, and API documentation
code_quality_signals.code_style: ESLint, Prettier for JavaScript/TypeScript, gofmt for Go code formatting

project_health.license: AGPL-3.0-only with Apache-2.0 exceptions for some components
project_health.recent_activity: Recent commits show active feature development including permissions improvements and frontend enhancements
project_health.community_signals: Comprehensive governance with CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, issue templates, security policy (SECURITY.md)

information_gaps: Exact database schema details - only migration file paths available
Complete list of supported data source plugins - would need full plugin directory analysis
Production performance characteristics - no load testing results visible

functional_dna.logic_archetype_evidence: System implements a comprehensive plugin architecture with TypeScript/Go SDKs for data sources and panels (pkg/plugins/), signature verification, and sandboxed execution. Core functionality extends through 150+ pluggable integrations with dynamic loading and standardized interfaces.
functional_dna.data_contract_evidence: REST API endpoints with structured JSON schemas, but dashboard definitions stored as flexible JSON documents. Plugin system allows arbitrary data source schemas while maintaining typed TypeScript interfaces for UI components.
functional_dna.core_algorithmic_evidence: System processes time-series queries with template variable substitution (pkg/tsdb/), implements real-time alerting rule evaluation, and provides correlation analysis between metric relationships. WebSocket streaming for live dashboard updates indicates temporal data processing patterns.
functional_dna.concurrency_evidence: Go goroutines with context-based cancellation, Redis caching layer, background scheduler for periodic tasks, and WebSocket streaming suggests eventual consistency model rather than strict ACID requirements.
functional_dna.abstract_problem: Aggregates heterogeneous data streams through pluggable adapters, applies configurable transformations and filtering rules, then renders the results in customizable visual layouts with real-time updates and conditional alerting.
functional_dna.data_flow_pattern: Multi-source ingestion → proxy/transform → cache/aggregate → real-time visualization with bidirectional WebSocket streaming for live updates
functional_dna.repurposing_reasoning: The plugin architecture, multi-tenant organization model, and configurable transformation pipeline are highly generic. The core pattern of aggregating disparate data sources through adapters and rendering in customizable layouts applies across many domains. However, time-series visualization components and alerting rules are somewhat specialized for monitoring use cases.

evidence_claims.claim: Implements pluggable data source architecture with 150+ integrations
evidence_claims.evidence: pkg/plugins/ directory structure and capability: 'Implements pluggable data source architecture supporting Prometheus, InfluxDB, CloudWatch, and 150+ integrations'

evidence_claims.claim: Uses Go goroutines with context-based cancellation for concurrency
evidence_claims.evidence: Architecture description: 'Go goroutines for concurrent request handling with context-based cancellation'

evidence_claims.claim: Provides real-time streaming via WebSocket connections
evidence_claims.evidence: API endpoint: 'GET /api/live/ws — WebSocket for live updates' and service: 'pkg/services/live/'

evidence_claims.claim: Stores dashboard definitions as JSON documents
evidence_claims.evidence: Data model: 'Dashboard - JSON document with panels, queries, and layout configuration' and schema pattern: 'Dashboard definitions stored as JSON documents'
Capability: 'Manages multi-tenant organizations with role-based access control and team permissions (pkg/services/user/, pkg/services/org/)'

cross_industry_applications: Financial Services - Trading desk dashboards aggregating market data feeds, risk metrics, and compliance reporting
Manufacturing - Industrial IoT sensor data visualization with production line monitoring and quality control alerts
Healthcare - Patient monitoring systems combining EHR data, medical device streams, and clinical workflow dashboards
Retail/E-commerce - Business intelligence platforms integrating sales data, inventory systems, and customer analytics
Smart Cities - Urban infrastructure monitoring combining traffic sensors, utility systems, and environmental data
Energy/Utilities - Grid monitoring systems aggregating generation, distribution, and consumption metrics with outage management
Logistics - Supply chain visibility platforms tracking shipments, warehouse operations, and delivery performance
Media/Entertainment - Content performance analytics combining streaming metrics, audience data, and revenue reporting

friction_report.migration_path: Extract plugin host framework and multi-source aggregation engine, replace time-series specific components with domain-appropriate data processors, customize visualization components for target use case

output.delivery: sync REST responses + async WebSocket streaming

authentication.method: OAuth2, LDAP, SAML, API keys with RBAC

comparable_systems: Apache Superset - Business intelligence platform aggregating multiple data sources into customizable dashboards
Tableau Server - Enterprise analytics platform with pluggable data connectors and real-time visualization
Splunk - Log aggregation and analysis platform with extensible apps and real-time search dashboards
Power BI - Microsoft's business analytics service with diverse data source integrations and collaborative dashboards
Elastic Kibana - Data visualization platform for Elasticsearch with plugin architecture and real-time monitoring
information_gaps: Exact plugin SDK API specifications and extension points
Database schema details for multi-tenant isolation implementation
WebSocket message protocols and streaming data formats
Query transformation engine architecture and optimization strategies
